message:
feat(server): add observability DB pool with archive-backed fallback and enrich TUI screens with identity normalization

Introduce ObservabilitySyncDb and ObservabilityDbPool abstractions that provide
read-only database access for server-side observability (TUI screens, startup
checks, tool metrics, static export) with automatic archive-snapshot fallback
when the live SQLite index is stale or locked.

Key changes:

- ObservabilitySyncDb: wraps a DbConn with optional SnapshotDirGuard for
  archive-backed snapshots; open_observability_sync_db_connection tries live
  SQLite first, falls back to archive reconstruction when drift is detected
- ObservabilityDbPool: wraps DbPool with snapshot lifecycle management for
  async read paths
- ArchiveDbDriftSummary and inspect_archive_db_drift centralize drift detection
  logic for the server layer
- TUI screens (agents, analytics, attachments, contacts, explorer, messages,
  projects, reservations, search, system_health, threads, timeline) updated to
  use open_observability_sync_db_connection instead of direct DbPoolConfig
  sqlite_path resolution, gaining archive fallback for free
- Agents screen: enrich_lossy_agent_summary_rows_from_previous preserves
  project scope across refreshes when the DB summary temporarily drops project
  metadata; should_preserve_previous_agent_rows prevents false empty state
- fetch_agent_rows_from_sqlite emits [unknown-project-N] / [unknown-agent-N] /
  [unknown-program] placeholder labels for rows with blank metadata
- query_palette_db_data merges agent metadata and recent messages into a single
  DB query function with graceful stale-cache fallback on error
- tui_poller gains archive-aware polling infrastructure
- mail_ui, startup_checks, static_export, tool_metrics updated for storage_root
  plumbing and observability pool usage

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
